name: Thai Curried Vegetables with Adzuki Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 red bell pepper, cut into strips
1 chayote fruit, cut into thin strips
1 cup bok choy, leaves, chopped (or swiss chard)
1 Thai eggplant, cut into thin strips (or half a small eggplant)
2 to 3 teaspoons red curry paste (Panang goong)
1 cup cooked adzuki beans, drained (or small red chili beans)
4 and a half cups hot cooked rice (3 to 4.5 cups)
Chopped fresh cilantro – or basil – optional garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a skillet or wok over medium heat.
2. Add the bell pepper, chayote squash, bok choy, mushrooms, and eggplant. Cook for 10 to 12 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Add the ginger and curry paste; stir-fry for 1 to 2 minutes more.
4. Stir in the coconut milk, soy sauce, lime juice, and beans. Bring to a simmer.
5. In a small mixing bowl, combine the cornstarch and warm water. Whisk this mixture into the coconut-curry mixture and continue to simmer for 2 more minutes, stirring frequently.
6. Serve over jasmine rice or brown rice.
Yield: 2 servings
Each serving provides: 374 Calories – 9 g fat (without rice)
